title: Fiskars PowerStep Pruner Anvil P83, Cutting diameter: 2.4 cm, Steel blades with non-stick coating, Length: 19 cm, Black/Orange, 1000575
availability_text: Only 9 left in stock (more on the way).
features: Anvil pruning shears for cutting dry hard growth, For branch sizes up to 2.4 cm (1'') in diameter, Suitable for cutting dry and hard growth, For right-handed people, For medium/large size hands

Optimal power transmission and reduced effort thanks to a 3-step PowerStep gradual cutting mechanism, Firm and comfortable grip with Fiberglass reinforced plastic handles FiberComp with non-slip coating
High quality manufacturing: non-stick coating on the upper blade for a smooth and clean upper cut
Long life product, High quality steel blades, Easy sharpening of the cutting blade, Diamond file sold separately in the maintenance kit (available under reference number 1001640), Finnish design
Contents: 1x Fiskars PowerStep Pruner Anvil P83, Length: 19 cm, Weight: 173 g, Material: High quality steel/fibreglass reinforced plastic, Colour: Black/Orange, 1000575
description: From the manufacturer At a glance: For dry branches and twigs up to 2.4 cm in dia. Innovative 3-step mechanism Ergonomically shaped handle Non-slip upper grip Non-stick coated upper blade PowerStep garden shears, Anvil, P83 For dry branches and twigs This anvil shear suits well when cutting dry and hard shoots and branches. This model is enhanced with an innovative 3-step mechanism, allowing you to adjust it quickly to wood of any thickness. The ergonomically shaped handles with a non-slip upper ensure a firm and highly comfortable grip. The non-stick coating on the upper blade ensures clean and precise cuts. Power Saving The innovative step mechanism on these PowerStep shears can be individually adjusted for branches and twigs of any thickness. This allows you to cut twigs or branches with a diameter of up to 2.4 centimetres without too much effort. Anvil - For dry wood Anvil-scissors only have one blade. The blade goes through the wood with pressure, supports the branch and distributes the pressure until the cut is finished. This cutting method is most suitable for dry, dead and hard woods. Not suitable for cutting fresh greenery. Bypass - For fresh wood The blade and counter blade cut into the wood and produce a clean cut without damaging the branch. This cutting method is most suitable for fresh plants, like roses or cuttings. Quality since 1649 Fiskars stands for quality and innovation since 1649. Our scissors have cut millions of metres of fabrics. Our axes have chopped millions of trunks. Our knives have cut millions of slices of bread. Our pans have cooked millions of marinated steaks. This all stands for 100% happiness.
